title: ampelygonum tinctorium
intro: Ampelygonum tinctorium, also known as New Zealand spinach or Tutu, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand. The plant's leaves have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Ampelygonum tinctorium include its ability to help manage symptoms of digestive issues, reduce pain and swelling, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
